# WandB run archive

Raw export of every training run from the original development period.

**This is a frozen artifact.** It is not regenerated by any user-facing
workflow. The script that produced it (`_export.py`) is kept here as
provenance only.

See [`../README.md`](../README.md) for context and curated run highlights.

## Layout

```
wandb/
├── README.md           this file
├── _export.py          the one-time script that produced everything below
├── index.json          machine-readable list of all 59 runs
├── summary.csv         spreadsheet-friendly digest (open in Excel/LibreOffice)
├── adhery-demo/        24 pharma-domain runs (project name unchanged from dev period)
│   └── <run_id>/
│       ├── config.yaml      run.config at launch
│       ├── summary.json     run.summary final values
│       └── history.parquet  full unsampled metric history
└── companion-plex/     35 companion-domain runs
    └── <run_id>/...    (same shape)
```

The original wandb projects were named `adhery-demo` and `companion-plex`
and we've preserved those names so cross-references in the historical
notes still resolve. The pharma-domain code in this repo was renamed
post-hoc; treat `adhery` in this archive as a synonym for `pharma`.

## Reading a run

```python
import pandas as pd, json, yaml

run_id = "0irtky6i"  # pharma v2-7, the first stable pharma run
base = f"docs/history/wandb/adhery-demo/{run_id}"

config = yaml.safe_load(open(f"{base}/config.yaml"))
summary = json.load(open(f"{base}/summary.json"))
history = pd.read_parquet(f"{base}/history.parquet")

print(history[["_step", "train/loss"]].tail())
```

## Browsing all runs

`summary.csv` has one row per run with: `project, id, name, state,
created_at, n_steps, url, tags`, plus the final-step values of
`train/loss`, `loss`, `eval/loss`, `eval_loss`, `train/lr`, `lr`,
`_step`, `_runtime`. Open it in any spreadsheet to skim all 59 runs at
once.

## Why parquet?

WandB's UI CSV export is sampled to ~500 points and capped. The
`run.scan_history()` API returns the full unsampled stream — these
parquets are what wandb actually stored. Total size is ~8 MB.
